title: spiraea chamaedryfolia subsp. flexuosa
intro: Spiraea chamaedryfolia subsp. flexuosa, also known as creeping spiraea, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of creeping spiraea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular remedy for colds, flu, and gastrointestinal problems. Creeping spiraea can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
